calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Mangini for (Administrator) Elizabeth Adler.

The Court stated that there are a lot of unclean aspects and that it has read the explanations. Mr. Mangini stated that he has sent the 13100 on two separate occasions to David Aldous with no response, that while notarization in Australia can be done by any government employee the nearest is over an hour drive. The Court asked about the $300.00 earning interest and Mr. Mangini clarified that it was dealt with when the estate in Australia was closed. The Court asked about the $3000.00 reserve requested in the proposed order. Mr. Mangini replied that it is for mailing expenses, the possible filing of a tax return and that they hope to complete distribution prior to the next bond payment being due. The Court asked about distribution to Mr. Aldous and Mr. Mangini replied that he would deposit it with the County Treasurer. The Court granted the petition for Final Distribution (subject to receipt/review of a revised order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Wu. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Continued To Jul-31-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Mangini for Elizabeth Adler (Administrator).

The Court stated that Counsel needs to provide a 13100 declaration from David Aldous, and that notarizations and certified death certificates are needed. Mr. Mangini stated that all of the heirs live in Australia, that he sent 13100 declarations to Mr. Aldous on two separate occasions, that he has not heard back from him and that he does not know if he will get one back from him. Mr. Mangini also stated that it is not easy to get Notary Publics out there, that twenty two people are sharing $35,919.00, that all but one of the Beneficiaries' share are under $2,000.00, that he has tried to get all of these documents, that it is extremely difficult and that he hopes the Court will waive in full the requirement of notarization. In addition, Mr. Mangini stated that he was unable to get certified copies of the death certificates, that they are only provided to family members, that he has requested them from the family, that they only provided copies and that he has a certified probate file for Doreen Stenhouse. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to July 31, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if Counsel can obtain any of the documents. If not, Counsel is to file a declaration explaining what efforts were made to obtain said documents.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
